comments.author: chatgpt-codex-connector
comments.body:
Codex usage limits have been reached for code reviews. Please check with the admins of this repo to increase the limits by adding credits.
Credits must be used to enable repository wide code reviews.

comments.author: linyueqian
comments.body:
To keep this moving while @Sy0307 is away, I pushed the gating discussed in the review directly to this branch (ede3dddf): the FINAL_ONLY coercion is now scoped to qwen3_tts full-payload deploys, and a new unit test pins that other async_chunk=false models keep DELTA coercion for streaming.

Verification on a Hopper-class GPU, vLLM 0.24.0: the serving coercion tests and the full worker runner test file pass (3 passed / 16 passed), and the --no-async-chunk streaming probe on the stock qwen3_tts yaml still shows byte-count parity with the non-streamed request (ratio 0.94, within run-to-run variance). ruff check and format are clean on the touched files.

The Low (flag source robustness) and Nit (unused seq_len parameter) from the review are left for a follow-up.

reviews.author: linyueqian
reviews.body:
Second-pass review at head fc40c1b2: code reading by two independent passes plus e2e probes on a Hopper-class GPU, vLLM 0.24.0 (probes ran at c523026a; the only change since is a merge of main that does not touch the files in this PR).

**The runner fix looks correct.** Switching the multimodal slice length to `scheduler_output.total_num_scheduled_tokens` fixes the dim-0 mismatch case where `to_payload_element` clones the whole batched tensor into every request payload, which is the cross-request contamination behind #4851. The two values coincide in the previously-working eager decode case and diverge exactly in the broken ones (graph-padded hidden states, and the async-snapshot empty-hidden case). The new worker test is meaningful; it fails without the fix by construction.

**Streaming probe of the serving change came back clean.** Both review passes independently predicted that FINAL_ONLY streaming would emit cumulative snapshot lists as duplicated audio, so I checked: booted this branch with `--no-async-chunk` on the stock `qwen3_tts.yaml` and compared `stream=True, stream_format="audio"` against `stream=False` for the same CustomVoice request. Streamed bytes were 0.9x the non-streamed run, within this branch's run-to-run length variance, so no duplicated or truncated audio on the raw streaming path for Qwen3-TTS.

Remaining comments are inline: one Med question about the coercion being global rather than gated per model type, one Low robustness note on the flag source, o
